line_map.line: Ship or sheep? (which one?) ih — ship! ee — sheep!
Hit or heat? Fit or feet? Sit or seat?
line_map.needs: acceptable-hold (abstract minimal-pair drill, handled by materials matching cards not book/song imagery)

line_map.line: Pin or pen? Hat or hut? Cat or cut? Bag or bug?
line_map.needs: acceptable-hold (same as above; pin/pen/hat/cat/bag/bug card images themselves are missing from folder - see generate list)

line_map.line: Bridge: A potato?! Ship? Sheep? NO!
Outro: Ship… sheep… (say it slow…) Which one? BOTH!
The sheep is on the ship! (hook, x2)
The ship is on the sea.
The sheep sits on a seat. (safe, sheep, safe!)
The wind! The ship can tip! Hold on, sheep!
Ship? Or sheep? (both! both!) The sheep is safe.
Outro: Not a ship… not a sheep… What is it?! (POTATO!)

wrong_art.file: sheep-sees-ship.png / sheep-on-ship.png / ship-on-sea.png / ship-tipping.png / sheep-on-seat.png / potato-between.png
wrong_art.issue: all six show a shaggy, matted, long-haired sheep texture that matches NEITHER sheep.png (realistic farm sheep) NOR ship-or-sheep.png (plush toy sheep) - Sheep is rendered in at least 3 different styles across this single 9-page review-week book, the worst cast-consistency break found across weeks 46-50
wrong_art.fix: regenerate all Sheep appearances in this book using ONE consistent plush-toy design matching the established cast look (fluffy round white plush, kind dark eyes, tan/cream face) as seen closest in ship-or-sheep.png; treat ship-or-sheep.png's sheep rendering as the reference to match
